steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Find point H on the circle
steps.content: Since $H=(-2,y)$ lies on the circle $x^{2}+y^{2}=4$: $$(-2)^{2}+y^{2}=4 \Rightarrow y^{2}=0 \Rightarrow y=0.$$ So $H=(-2,0)$, directly opposite $F=(2,0)$.

steps.title: Determine the geometric angle
steps.content: The rays $GF$ and $GH$ point in opposite directions along the x-axis, so the central angle is $\pi$ radians. Any coterminal positive measure has the form $$\pi + 2\pi k = (2k+1)\pi,$$ i.e., an odd multiple of $\pi$.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
We need an odd positive integer multiple of $\pi$.
  • A) $\frac{31\pi}{2}=15.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of $\pi$.
  • B) $\frac{33\pi}{2}=16.5\pi$ — not an integer multiple of $\pi$.
  • C) $28\pi$ — even multiple, gives angle $0$, not $\pi$.
  • D) $29\pi = 14(2\pi)+\pi$ — odd multiple, coterminal with $\pi$.
D is correct.
